heading: Helium lands subject to entry
label: 529 Helium lands subject to entry
text: Lands withdrawn from the public domain which are within (a) Helium Reserve Numbered 1, pursuant to Executive orders of , and , and (b) Helium Reserve Numbered 2 pursuant to Executive Order 6184 of , shall be subject to entry and location under the mining laws of the United States, and to permit and lease under the mineral leasing laws, upon determination by the Secretary of the Interior, based upon available geologic and other information, that there is no reasonable probability that operations pursuant to entry or location of the particular lands under the mining laws, or pursuant to a permit or lease of the particular lands under the Mineral Leasing Act [ et seq.], will result in the extraction or cause loss or waste of the helium-bearing gas in the lands of such reserves: , That the lands shall not become subject to entry, location, permit or lease until such time as the Secretary designates in an order published in the Federal Register: , That the Secretary may at any time as a condition to continued mineral operations require the entryman, locator, permittee or lessee to take such measures either above or below the surface of the lands as the Secretary deems necessary to prevent loss or waste of the helium-bearing gas.March 21, 1924January 28, 1926June 26, 193330 U.S.C. 181ProvidedAnd provided further
notes: References in TextThe Mineral Leasing Act, referred to in text, is , , which is classified generally to chapter 3A (§ 181 et seq.) of this title.
